methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.3B - $126.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Scale AI, a private AI & Software company founded by Alexandr Wang and Lucy Guo, is valued at $29B following $15.9B in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of eight same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables spanning private and one public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR), with valuations ranging from $8.3B to $126.0B.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach for this analysis, yields an implied valuation of $14.5B based on the median across the eight-company peer set. The sample includes Waymo at $126.0B (Pre-IPO), Figure AI at $39.0B (Series C), Reflection AI at $25.0B (Series A), Applied Intuition at $15.0B (Series A), and Skild AI at $14.0B (Series C), among others. This single-method triangulation produces a low estimate of $12.3B, a median of $14.5B, and a high estimate of $16.7B.

The current $29B valuation sits substantially above the peer-derived range of $12.3B–$16.7B, reflecting either a significant premium relative to observed comparables or divergent market pricing of Scale AI's stage and capital intensity. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity within the peer set (stage dispersion from Series A to Pre-IPO and valuation spread of over 15x), which constrains precision. Additional methods incorporating revenue multiples or forward-looking metrics would strengthen conviction in any valuation conclusion.
